input_row.title: Ensure 'FTP state' is set to 'FTPS only' or 'Disabled'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment Slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, set FTP state to FTPS only or Disabled. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to set FTPS state to FtpsOnly or Disabled: az resource update --name web --resource-group <resource-group-name> -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites/<function-app- name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --set properties.ftpsState=<FtpsOnly|Disabled> Default Value: By default, FTP state is set to FTPS only. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/deploy-ftp 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-security 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-how-to-use- azure-function-app-settings#ftps-deployment-settings 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- data-protection#dp-4-encrypt-sensitive-information-in-transit 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities CIS Controls:
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/deploy-ftp 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-security 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-how-to-use- azure-function-app-settings#ftps-deployment-settings 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- data-protection#dp-4-encrypt-sensitive-information-in-transit 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities CIS Controls: Controls Version Control IG 1 IG 2 IG 3 v8 3.10 Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH). ● ● v7 14.4 Encrypt All Sensitive Information in Transit Encrypt all sensitive information in transit.  ● ●

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit approach is determined to be Automated because:
- The command provided in the remediation suggests the use of Azure CLI to check and update the configuration of the FTP state.
- Azure Resource Manager (ARM) APIs can be used to query the properties related to App Services and Function Apps, including the FTPS state.
- The control's nature, focusing on configuration settings that can be directly managed and queried via API, aligns with the criteria for an Automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_function_app_deployment_slot_ftps_state_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:
- Use Azure CLI commands to enumerate all Function Apps and their deployment slots within the specified subscription and resource groups.
- Specifically, check the configuration property `ftpsState` for each deployment slot.
- The expected values for compliance are either `"FtpsOnly"` or `"Disabled"`.

**Exact implementation details:**
1. **Enumeration of Resources:** Use the command:
   ```
   az functionapp list --resource-group <resource-group-name> --query "[].{name:name, slots:deploymentSlots}" --output json
   ```
   to retrieve the list of function apps and their respective deployment slots.

2. **Check FTPS State:** For each deployment slot, run the command:
   ```
   az resource show --name <function-app-name> --resource-group <resource-group-name> --namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites/<function-app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --query "properties.ftpsState" --output tsv
   ```
   to retrieve the current FTPS state configuration.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** The command returns either "FtpsOnly" or "Disabled".
   - **Fail:** The command returns any value other than those specified.

4. An example API endpoint that might be used for verifying this property could be similar to:
   ```
   GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/{functionAppName}/slots/{slotName}/config/web?api-version=2019-08-01
   ```
   where the response body contains the `ftpsState` property.

This automated approach will efficiently verify compliance with the specified control by leveraging Azure APIs directly to ensure secure settings are in place.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of Azure CLI and APIs is appropriate for verifying the control. The technical details are robust and align with Azure's capabilities.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Simplify the program name for clarity and ensure error handling is included in the automation script.
- The overall approach and technical implementation are sound, so no reclassification is necessary. The focus should be on refining the implementation details for robustness and maintainability.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach should be automated, and this aligns with Azure's capabilities. The `ftpsState` property for Azure Function Apps and their deployment slots can be queried and managed using Azure CLI and APIs, which supports an automated approach. The control's focus on configuration settings that are accessible via these tools further justifies automation.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_functionapp_deployment_slot_ftps_state_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure Resource/Service:** Azure Function Apps
- **API/CLI Method:** `az functionapp config show` or equivalent ARM API calls
- **Properties to Check:** `ftpsState` property within the configuration settings
- **Pass Criteria:** The `ftpsState` property is set to `"FtpsOnly"` or `"Disabled"`
- **Fail Criteria:** The `ftpsState` property is set to any value other than `"FtpsOnly"` or `"Disabled"`

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more concise program name, which has been adopted.
